input.headline: Working from home in sectional title units: Know the rules to avoid eviction

raw.headline: Working from home in sectional title units: Know the rules to avoid eviction

winner: Why did the WFH employee bring a ladder to the communal garden? To *section* the unit into "work from home" and "eviction zones.

candidates.text: My boss told me I could work from home forever—turns out he meant *his* couch, not mine. Eviction letter arrived today with a coffee stain.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with coffee stain adds unexpected detail; concise and specific, aligns well with headline context."}

candidates.text: If you're working from home in a sectional title unit, make sure your Zoom call doesn't accidentally broadcast the neighbor's yoga session — or you might find yourself evicted for being a passive-aggressive background dancer.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the topic. It uses a relatable scenario (Zoom calls) with a humorous twist involving passive-aggressive background dancing, which adds funniness while staying relevant to the headline."}

candidates.text: I was so worried about being evicted for working from home in my sectional title unit that I spent all day studying the bylaws, only to realize I’d been violating the "no unauthorized pets" rule by keeping my laptop on my lap.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and naturally funny"}

candidates.text: My sectional title committee is considering evicting me because I tried to set up my home office in the common hallway, claiming it was my "private workspace," but they argued that a hallway is technically just a long room where everyone is forced to work together.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the topic, with a clever twist on the concept of 'private workspace' in a common area."}

candidates.text: Why did the remote worker get evicted? Because they kept saying, "I'm just sectioning out my time!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with wordplay on 'sectional title units' and 'sectioning out my time,' offering surprise and specificity. However, it's slightly forced and could be more concise for maximum impact."}

candidates.text: Why did the WFH employee bring a ladder to the communal garden? To *section* the unit into "work from home" and "eviction zones.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'section' and the context of sectional title units. It's specific to the topic and has a surprising twist, though the explanation feels slightly forced."}

candidates.text: If you think working from home in a sectional title unit is just about Wi-Fi and coffee, be prepared for a surprise — your landlord might show up with a eviction notice and a spreadsheet.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the topic, with a clever twist involving a spreadsheet. It adheres to natural English but could be more original."}

candidates.text: Working from home in sectional title units: know the rules to avoid eviction. "Make sure your Zoom call doesn't accidentally become a lease agreement — your landlord might just come knocking with a contract and a sense of humor.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, slightly surprising, and ties the topic to a relatable situation (Zoom calls). However, the humor is mild and could be more specific or punchier."}

candidates.text: Working from home in a sectional title unit is risky because if you don't follow the rules, you might get evicted for being a bit too "remote" a property.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'remote' meaning both distant and not in person, but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about sectional title units or eviction rules. The humor is mild and could be more surprising."}
